changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_privileged_user_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_privileged_user_actions_logged for better clarity and consistency with audit logging resource
review_notes: The functions accurately map the FedRAMP AC-2 (7) control to Kubernetes RBAC and audit logging features. The original naming for the audit logging function was slightly misleading as it implied RBAC was responsible for audit logging, which is not the case. The revised name better reflects the audit logging resource and its purpose.
naming_improvements: Ensure audit-related functions use the 'audit' resource prefix for clarity
title: Account Management | Privileged User Accounts
step1_reasoning: The control AC-2 (7) focuses on managing privileged user accounts, which can be mapped to Kubernetes RBAC controls. Ensuring that there are no unnecessary cluster-admin bindings and enforcing least privilege are direct mappings. Additionally, auditing privileged user actions can be achieved through Kubernetes audit logging, ensuring that actions by users with elevated privileges are logged.
